name: A. Maldonado (Child & Family Resources)
subject_line: Data: Arizona's pedestrian fatality rate is 3.6 per 100
personalization_paragraph: Arizona's pedestrian fatality rate is 3.6 per 100,000 residents, nearly twice the national average; 76 percent of pedestrian deaths happen in darkness on wide arterials designed for vehicle speed, not pedestrian safety. The data file pairs the headline finding with the named-corridor breakdown, methodology, and the raw records behind it.
